title: C4OS Grill Question 002: Native Browser Scope
description: Answer this one grill question locally, submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: What does full native Browser mean for the first MVP?
options: Local preview surface with URL bar, reload, stop, and open-external controls only
Native Browser can navigate arbitrary web URLs, but no login persistence, downloads, cookies, or privileged bridge
Full web browsing with cookies/login sessions/downloads, still isolated from C4OS secrets and privileged APIs
Browser automation and agent-controlled browsing are required in MVP
questions.help: Current proof risk: direct Tauri WebviewWindow exposed Tauri internals to page content. Recommended answer: arbitrary URL navigation only if implemented with an isolated surface that has no privileged bridge, no downloads, and no persistent logged-in session behavior in MVP.

questions.label: Why is this Browser scope required in the first MVP?
questions.help: Give the customer workflow that breaks if Browser is only a constrained local preview or if Browser is deferred.
questions.placeholder: Example: The agent must run a local app and the user must inspect it in-app without switching to Chrome.

questions.label: Which Browser risks are acceptable for MVP?
options: No persistent cookies or logged-in sessions
No privileged C4OS bridge in page content
Open unknown URLs externally instead of in-app
All of these constraints are too restrictive
questions.help: Select the constraints you can accept in exchange for shipping Browser in MVP.

questions.label: Clarifications or constraints
questions.help: Add any URL policy, localhost behavior, tab behavior, external browser handoff, or isolation requirement you want recorded.
